title: Life before the scan
summary: Before cancer was part of my vocabulary, life was already full: travel, love, work, plans, and the quiet assumption that tomorrow would mostly resemble today.
body: I want this story to start here because the diagnosis did not create my life. It interrupted one that was already moving. These are the images I want people to hold in their head before the scan reports and treatment names arrive.

media.caption: Life before the diagnosis.
Life before cancer entered the story.
A life already in motion.

title: One word in an app: lesion
summary: I found out by email while I was at dinner with my wife. I opened the app, saw the word lesion, and had to Google what it meant.
body: The strange part was that I had no symptoms. A whole-body MRI had unexpectedly found a non-enhancing right frontal lesion, and one unfamiliar word turned into one of the biggest decisions of my life: how to find the right doctor when everything was suddenly on the line.

media.caption: Axial FLAIR-style brain MRI clip.
Personal image from the early diagnosis window.

title: I built a doctor search engine
summary: I treated finding a brain surgeon like a system: broad search, human signal, then real consults.
body: I made a sheet for the logistics everyone kept asking for, then another for the real question: who had actually spent years on this exact condition? I started with 23 doctors, added signals like publications, clinical trials, specialization, and reputation inside medicine, then narrowed the list through conversations and consults.
facts: Decision rule: choose honesty over comfort

media.caption: Illustration of the doctor-search system: records, signals, cities, and consults.

title: Right frontal craniotomy and gross total resection
summary: Gross total resection of a WHO Grade 2 diffuse astrocytoma.
body: The operative and pathology records cluster around September 1 to September 5, 2023. For the public timeline, this is treated as one major event: right frontal craniotomy with gross total resection and pathology confirming WHO Grade 2 diffuse astrocytoma.

media.caption: Short artifact from the surgery date.

title: The postoperative chapter begins
summary: Serial notes describe a fluid-filled postoperative cavity and minimal surrounding T2/FLAIR signal.
body: The medical record language is stable and technical. The lived experience is not. This section is where recovery images and short reflections can carry what the scan report cannot.

media.caption: Recovery after surgery.
The early postoperative chapter.

title: Stable postoperative bed
summary: Residual fluid-filled postoperative bed; minimal wispy peri-resection enhancement unchanged.
body: No masslike enhancement was reported. This becomes the rhythm of the timeline: surveillance, stability, and the work of learning what stable means.

media.caption: Illustration of stable MRI surveillance and watchful waiting.

title: Portugal, trying to enjoy life
summary: After surgery and early surveillance, we went to Portugal and tried to let life be bigger than the diagnosis.
body: There was still fear in the background, but I did not want every month to become a medical month. Portugal was me trying to enjoy the life I still had in front of me, even while the next decisions were starting to form.

media.caption: Portugal, trying to enjoy life between appointments.

title: The Camino trips
summary: Walking became its own kind of answer: not a cure, not a medical milestone, but a way to keep moving through uncertainty.
body: The Camino trips belong here in the timeline, before the IDH-inhibitor and vaccine chapters accelerate. After surgery, inside surveillance, and before the next wave of treatment planning, I wanted to feel my body moving forward through the world.
facts: A personal milestone, not a medical endpoint

media.caption: Camino moment from the personal timeline.
A travel moment from the Camino chapter.
On the Camino route.
Another Camino route image.
Illustration of walking the Camino while still inside the treatment story.

title: Ivosidenib begins
summary: The records place the ivosidenib start in late June 2024 as the first IDH inhibitor chapter.
body: The treatment lane starts to carry a new kind of story: targeted therapy, monitoring, adverse effects, and switching strategies when the body objects.

media.caption: Illustration of targeted IDH therapy and careful monitoring.

title: Vaccine priming starts in Tübingen
summary: The first personalized peptide vaccine administrations began July 9-12, 2024.
body: This corrects the timeline: the vaccine story starts in July 2024. The first four administrations happened across July 9, 10, 11, and 12 with Leukine and Imiquimod, with the July 12 visit also used for immune-monitoring blood draw.

media.caption: Illustration of the personal neoantigen vaccine priming phase.

title: Ivosidenib discontinued
summary: Ivosidenib stopped because of chest pain / angina documentation.
body: This event should eventually include the short version of what happened and how the decision was made. It belongs in the treatment lane, but the human part matters too.

media.caption: Illustration of treatment adjustment after an adverse-effect signal.

title: We started building Triangle
summary: Triangle started in September 2024, after we raised the money to turn the patient-navigation process I had built for myself into a real product.
body: The idea came from living the problem: fragmented records, medical jargon, treatment options buried across papers and trials, and the unfair advantage of knowing how to work the system. September 2024 was when that personal operating system became the beginning of the Triangle build.
facts: A product version of the system I needed

media.caption: Early Triangle build moment.
Illustration of the patient-navigation system turning into a product build.

title: Vorasidenib starts
summary: Vorasidenib 40 mg daily began as the alternative IDH inhibitor strategy.
body: This becomes one of the ongoing therapy anchors, with later LFT monitoring and temporary hold/resume events. In the deepdive, this is the targeted-therapy half of the treatment idea: suppress the mutant IDH biology while the immune strategy tries to teach T cells what to see.

media.caption: Illustration of ongoing IDH inhibition and monitoring.

title: First clear vaccine-response signal
summary: Four-month monitoring detected CD4+ responses to 5 of 17 peptides and CD8+ responses to 4 of 17 peptides.
body: The report summarized this as specific T-cell response to 8 of the 17 vaccinated mutation-spanning peptides. This is the key early immune-readout moment: not proof of clinical benefit, but evidence that the immune system had measurable responses to parts of the vaccine cocktail.

media.caption: Illustration of measured T-cell response to vaccine peptides.

title: No masslike enhancing recurrence
summary: MRI brain reported no residual or recurrent masslike enhancement.
body: This is a good example of the timeline's tone: calm, precise, and not over-celebrating. Stable is meaningful.

media.caption: Illustration of surveillance imaging showing stability.

title: The tumor gets a molecular identity
summary: This is when the cancer stopped being only a scan and a grade for me. It became IDH1 R132C, ATRX, TP53, MGMT, TMB, MSI, and a set of clues I could actually study.
body: Getting the molecular picture changed the way I understood my cancer. The diagnosis was still personal and terrifying, but it also became something with structure: a pattern of mutations, biomarkers, and uncertainty that could guide research, second opinions, and the treatment strategy.

media.caption: Illustration of the tumor's core molecular identity.

title: Ninth vaccine dose while clinically well
summary: CeCurio documented the 9th personalized peptide vaccination on March 10, 2025.
body: The visit note recorded ECOG 0 and KPS 100%, no reported disease symptoms, current vorasidenib treatment, and last MRI without signs of tumor in December 2024. The vaccine was administered with Leukine and Imiquimod.

media.caption: Illustration of ongoing personal neoantigen vaccine dosing.

title: Vorasidenib held for elevated LFTs
summary: ALT/AST elevations led to a temporary vorasidenib hold.
body: The lab lane should make the invisible parts visible: monitoring, thresholds, holds, and restarts. This event can eventually include a tiny ALT/AST sparkline.

media.caption: Illustration of medication monitoring and lab-guided treatment hold.

title: Ten-month vaccine response persists
summary: Ten months after the first vaccine, immune monitoring detected responses to 9 of 18 vaccinated mutation-spanning peptides.
body: The June 23, 2025 immune-monitoring report described CD4+ T-cell responses to 5 of 18 tested peptides and CD8+ T-cell responses to 6 of 18 tested peptides. This makes the vaccine story more than a design idea: it has longitudinal immune readouts.

media.caption: Illustration of persistent vaccine-linked immune responses.

title: Vorasidenib resumed
summary: Vorasidenib resumed after improvement in liver function tests.
body: The treatment story is not linear; it is adjusted, watched, resumed, and re-checked.

media.caption: Illustration of restarting vorasidenib after lab improvement.

title: One-year vaccine response still detectable
summary: One year and one month after the first vaccine, specific T-cell responses to at least 5 of 18 vaccinated mutation-spanning peptides were still detectable.
body: The report also noted low measured CD4+/CD8+ cell numbers and/or viability after in-vitro culture and recommended repeating the assay. That caveat belongs beside the result: still detectable, but with sample-quality limits.

media.caption: Illustration of one-year immune monitoring after the personal neoantigen vaccine.

title: Triangle steps into the world
summary: The company story became public: a patient-built AI health tool shaped by the diagnosis and the system I wished had existed.
body: By this point, Triangle had already been underway since September 2024. The announcement made the origin story public: complete records, AI-powered research, and a doctor in the loop, built from the experience of having to become an expert in my own disease.

media.caption: Illustration of Triangle moving from personal system to public product.

title: The doctor-search system became the deeper lesson
summary: The surgeon search made one thing obvious: the healthcare system works best for people who already know how to work the system.
body: I started with a simple sheet, then turned it into a search process: broad list, publication and trial signal, reputation inside medicine, and consults that showed me how each doctor thought. The final decision rule was not who made me feel safest in the moment. It was who corrected me, pushed back, and helped me make the right decision.

media.caption: Illustration of the doctor-search framework that later shaped Triangle.

title: Stable on surveillance
summary: Ongoing follow-up documents clinical stability and stable MRI appearance while on vorasidenib and immunotherapy.
body: The current-state card should be the quiet ending of the scroll: stable imaging, improved LFTs, and the next part still unfolding.
facts: No masslike or nodular enhancing recurrence reported
ALT/AST improved relative to prior peaks

media.caption: Illustration of continued stable surveillance.

title: The thing I needed is now a real product
summary: The ending for now is simple: Triangle is on screen.
body: The system I wished I had during diagnosis has become a product other patients can use.

media.caption: Triangle home with health topics and patient context.
Triangle genome view showing body-system genetic context.
